mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary overheating, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales rise alongside CPIAUCSL increases greater than 0.5 percent monthly, or interaction Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when annual GDP growth exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when payrolls rise but unemployment stagnates, or interaction Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts signal weakening residential investment and reduced construction activity, which drags on broader economic growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal becomes positive when HOUST drops below 800 thousand units.
